basics.name: Jaime Andrés Quiceno González
basics.label: Software Architect · Tech Lead · DevOps
basics.summary: Software Architect with 15+ years of experience building products, automations and scalable platforms for companies such as Knowmad Mood, Conversia, Sportium, Wolters Kluwer, Green Power Monitor and Vueling. Focused on .NET, Angular, Azure, AWS and DevOps for enterprise environments.

work.name: Knowmad Mood
work.summary: Requirements analysis, architecture design and framework evolution for the corporate architecture team.
highlights: Framework evolution for the architecture team
Implementation of new capabilities for enterprise software environments

work.name: Conversia
work.summary: Architecture design, Azure CI/CD, SonarQube, Checkmarx, Terraform, Ansible, Docker, AWS and cross-company technical enablement.
highlights: Worked with SonarQube, Checkmarx, Terraform, Ansible and Docker

work.name: Sportium / Capitole Consulting
work.summary: Feature delivery and architecture support for an online betting platform using microservices, Kafka and WebSockets.
highlights: Worked with microservices, Kafka and WebSockets
Used .NET, Angular and DevOps practices in a high-demand online platform

work.name: Vueling Airlines
work.summary: Worked in Agile teams delivering features with .NET, AWS, Azure Cloud CI, Docker, Blazor and engineering best practices.
highlights: Built new functionality in Agile teams
Worked with AWS, Azure Cloud CI and Docker
Used Blazor and .NET in enterprise airline systems

work.name: Industrias Electromecánicas Magnetron
work.summary: Automation of production processes through barcodes, achieving few steps to process information.
highlights: Improved information processing with barcode workflows
Delivered .NET solutions in a manufacturing environment

highlights: Designed and developed new features in Java
Worked with Spring, Hibernate and Oracle
Built geolocation solutions for an industrial transport use case
